# Lovable Stack Architect

> Side-by-side comparator that helps founders, PMs, designers and developers pick the best backend stack for a Lovable-built app. Free, open-source community template.

Independent, community-built template by @brettadler (https://lovable.dev/@brettadler) — not affiliated with, endorsed by, or representing Lovable.


Lovable Stack Architect scores 10 popular hosting and backend platforms across 12 criteria — time-to-launch, DX with Lovable, cost at small and large scale, scaling ceiling, realtime, storage, AI/heavy compute, compliance, vendor lock-in, ops burden, and migration path. Inputs (stage, expected MAU, team strengths, budget, compliance needs, workloads, lock-in tolerance, time-to-market priority) drive a ranked recommendation, side-by-side matrix, architecture diagram, and exportable report. Every criterion starts at the same baseline weight; the recommendation card surfaces which criteria drove the top score and what trade-off you make versus the runner-up.

Platforms covered: Lovable Cloud, external Supabase, Vercel, Netlify, AWS, Google Cloud, Azure, Heroku, Render, Fly.io. Only Lovable Cloud and external Supabase are native Lovable integrations; the other 8 options assume you export your Lovable project to GitHub and deploy the backend yourself.

## Pages

- [Home](/): Marketing landing — what the tool does, example scenarios, FAQ, and a path to remix it as a template.
- [Tool](/app): The full comparator — inputs, ranked recommendation with rationale and runner-up trade-off, side-by-side matrix, cost estimate with sources, and architecture diagram. Share scenarios via URL; export Markdown or PDF.
- [Methodology](/methodology): How the score is computed, the rubric and weights, cost-band sources per platform, and the tool's known biases.

## How to remix

This site is a free MIT-licensed template. The whole comparator is data-driven — edit `src/data/architectures.ts` (options, criteria, 1–5 rubric scores) and `src/lib/scoring.ts` (weights) to re-aim it at any decision space: CMS picker, database picker, auth picker, AI-model picker, anything. See `TEMPLATE-SETUP.md` in the repo for the full rebrand checklist (constants, HTML head, favicons, OG image, copy).
